basics.name: Faye Pourghasem
basics.label: AI Developer & Researcher
basics.summary: AI developer shipping production GenAI and agentic systems into regulated, customer-facing environments. MASc from University of Toronto in Biomedical Engineering (AI for Clinical Systems). Hands-on with LLM fine-tuning, Google ADK, Vertex AI, LangGraph, and MCP.

work.name: Canadian Institute for Health Information (CIHI)
work.summary: Building production ML systems on 10+ TB of national healthcare data consumed by internal leadership and the WHO.
highlights: Shipped production XGBoost models predicting healthcare staff shortages across 13 provinces; containerized on Databricks with MLflow-tracked experiments.
Architected Airflow DAGs integrating 5+ legacy health data silos, reducing ingestion-to-model latency by 70% under PHIPA governance.
Built evaluation and observability frameworks tracking prediction drift, input validity, and latency across weekly batch cycles.

work.name: University Health Network (UHN) — KITE Research Institute
work.summary: Built clinical AI systems end-to-end, including RAG pipelines, computer vision, and conversational agents for healthcare.
highlights: Triage Avatar: Architected a production RAG pipeline powering a conversational LLM agent for structured patient intake with hallucination guardrails and voice-to-text ingestion.
AVA (Lead, $250K): Built a real-time computer vision pipeline using MediaPipe for pose estimation, deployed on React + Azure in a clinical setting; co-authored 2 first-author publications.

education.area: Biomedical Engineering — AI for Clinical Systems

awards.title: 4th Place — Google Cloud Hackathon
awards.summary: Built a multi-agent event planner with Google ADK, Vertex AI, LangGraph, and MCP-driven tool routing in 24 hours.

awards.title: 1st Prize — AGE-WELL Summer Institute
awards.summary: Co-founded AgeUnity, a personalized recommendation system for older adults. Led technical architecture across a 5-person team.

publications.name: The use of digital avatars to improve virtual rehabilitation health outcomes amongst adults with health conditions: a scoping review
publications.summary: First-author scoping review on digital avatars for virtual rehabilitation.

publications.name: Feasibility Pilot Testing of an AI-based Virtual Assistant among Healthy Adults to Support Virtual Rehabilitation

projects.name: Multi-Agent Event Planner
projects.description: Planner/executor multi-agent system on Google ADK and Vertex AI with hierarchical delegation via MCP-driven tool routing. ReAct reasoning with ChromaDB-backed RAG.
highlights: 4th of 20 teams at Google Cloud Hackathon

projects.name: AgeUnity
projects.description: Personalized recommendation system matching users by interests and proximity. Led technical architecture and secured Mitacs Accelerate funding.
